summary: Threat hunting report portal based on MITRE ATT&CK framework with PDF generation capability
known_issues: Free transform tool not properly resizing icons and text labels
Load diagram functionality has issues with date/time display
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Simple user profile icon illustration in black and white, showing a person silhouette (user-icon.dim_128x128.png)
- Single desktop computer icon illustration in black and white, showing one computer monitor and base unit (computer-icon.dim_128x128.png)

existing_features.title: Create a threat hunting report form interface where users can input threat hunting details including report title, date, executive summary, threat actors, techniques observed, indicators of compromise (IOCs), and findings
Integrate MITRE ATT&CK framework data by providing a searchable dropdown or selection interface for MITRE ATT&CK techniques with their IDs (e.g., T1059.001) and descriptions
Implement PDF generation functionality that creates a professionally formatted threat hunting report containing all report sections including MITRE ATT&CK techniques, IOCs, and findings
Create a report listing page that displays all saved threat hunting reports with basic information (title, date, techniques count) and allows users to view full details or generate PDFs

Design a professional, security-focused visual theme with a cohesive color palette (avoiding blue and purple), clear typography optimized for readability of technical content, and a layout that emphasizes structured information and data clarity suitable for cybersecurity professionals

Extend the MITRE ATT&CK technique selector to support custom text input in addition to the searchable dropdown, allowing users to add techniques not in the predefined list or add custom notes to existing techniques

Expand the report form with additional detailed fields commonly needed for comprehensive threat hunting reports, including: Timeline of Events, Attack Vector Analysis, Evidence Collection Details, Affected Systems/Assets, Remediation Actions Taken, and Recommendations for Future Prevention

Update the PDF generation functionality to include all new detailed threat hunting fields (Timeline of Events, Attack Vector Analysis, Evidence Collection, Affected Systems, Remediation Actions, Recommendations) and properly render custom MITRE technique entries
Create a new page route '/attack-path' for visualizing attack paths with a canvas or interactive workspace where users can create visual diagrams
Implement a set of pre-defined draggable icon components representing attack path elements: email, attacker, computer, server, domain, IP address, and file
Enable users to drag and position attack path icons freely on the canvas to create visual attack path diagrams
Allow users to draw connecting lines or arrows between icons to illustrate the flow and sequence of attack events
Add navigation link to the attack path visualization page in the main header navigation alongside existing report pages
Implement freehand drawing capability on the attack path canvas allowing users to draw freely with mouse or pointer like a paint application
Add drawing tools to the attack path interface including the ability to draw lines and arrows as freehand elements separate from icon connections
Remove the IP address icon from the attack path visualization toolbar and prevent it from being placed on the canvas
Add an eraser tool to the attack path visualizer toolbar that allows users to remove drawn elements (freehand strokes, lines, arrows) by clicking on them
Implement an undo function in the attack path visualizer that reverses the most recent action (icon placement, connection creation, drawing stroke, or deletion)
Add a text tool to the attack path visualizer that allows users to type and place custom text labels anywhere on the canvas
Update the useAttackPathState hook to track action history and support undo operations for all attack path elements (icons, connections, drawings, text labels)

Add eight new file type icons to the attack path visualizer toolbar: .exe, .dll, .pdf, .ppt, .csv, .zip, .doc, and command and control (C2). Each icon should be draggable and placeable on the canvas like existing attack path elements.
Rename the existing 'file' icon type to 'file/folder' throughout the attack path visualizer, including the toolbar label, icon component, and state management
Change the attack path canvas background color from its current color to white
Add a color picker interface to the text tool in the attack path visualizer allowing users to select a color for text labels before or after placing them
Implement JPEG export functionality that captures the entire attack path canvas (including all icons, connections, drawings, and text labels) and downloads it as a JPEG image file
Implement frontend functionality to save the current attack path diagram state to the backend, including a save button in the attack path visualizer toolbar and handling for save operations

Implement frontend functionality to load previously saved attack path diagrams from the backend, including a load/open interface that displays available saved diagrams and restores the complete canvas state when selected
Implement a color picker interface in the attack path visualizer text tool that allows users to select between black and red colors for text labels
Add IP address icon back to the attack path visualizer toolbar as a draggable element that can be placed on the canvas
Fix the eraser function in the attack path visualizer to properly remove all types of elements (icons, connections, drawings, text labels) when clicked
Implement Internet Identity authentication with a login page that protects the application and requires users to authenticate before accessing any features
Implement frontend save functionality in the attack path visualizer that captures the current canvas state and persists it to the backend, including a save button or interface element

Implement frontend load functionality in the attack path visualizer that retrieves saved diagrams from the backend and displays them in a selection interface, restoring the complete canvas state when a diagram is selected
Convert the .exe and .dll icons in the attack path visualizer to black and white styling
Add a script icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar as a draggable element that can be placed on the canvas
Update the eraser tool to remove all element types including icons, connections, drawings, and text labels when clicked
Enable drawing tool elements (freehand strokes, lines, arrows) to be movable after creation
Replace the .csv icon with an Excel icon in the attack path visualizer
Replace the .doc icon with a Microsoft Word icon in the attack path visualizer
Change the IP address icon to display as text reading 'IP Address' instead of a graphical icon
Add an up and down parallel arrow (bidirectional vertical arrow) icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar as a draggable element that can be placed on the canvas
Enable text label rotation in the attack path visualizer, allowing users to rotate text labels to any angle
Implement a free transform tool in the attack path visualizer that allows users to move and resize both icons and text labels with visual transformation controls
Change the .exe icon in the attack path visualizer from a graphical icon to a text-based representation displaying '.exe'
Change the Excel icon in the attack path visualizer to display as text reading 'Microsoft Excel' instead of a graphical icon
Change the Word icon in the attack path visualizer to display as text reading 'Microsoft Word' instead of a graphical icon
Remove the IP Address icon from the attack path visualizer toolbar and prevent it from being placed on the canvas
Add diagram naming functionality when saving attack path diagrams in the attack path visualizer, prompting users to input a name for the diagram before saving
Implement diagram selection interface in the load functionality to display and choose from multiple saved diagrams by name
Add a clear button to the attack path visualizer toolbar that removes all elements from the canvas (icons, connections, drawings, text labels)
Remove the JPEG export functionality and replace it with PNG export functionality that captures the entire attack path canvas and downloads it as a PNG image file
Fix the free transform tool to properly move and resize both icons and text labels with working transformation controls
Remove the bidirectional arrow icon from the attack path visualizer toolbar and prevent it from being placed on the canvas
Update the free transform tool in the attack path visualizer to properly resize icons in addition to text labels, ensuring transformation handles work correctly for both element types
Add delete functionality to the load diagram interface allowing users to remove saved diagrams from backend storage
Fix the eraser tool in the attack path visualizer to properly delete icons when clicked, in addition to its existing ability to remove connections, drawings, and text labels
Fix the PNG export functionality in the attack path visualizer to properly capture and download the entire canvas including all icons, connections, drawings, and text labels as a PNG image file

Fix the free transform tool in AttackPathCanvas.tsx to properly resize icons when transformation handles are dragged, ensuring the width and height of placed icons update correctly with the visual resize controls

Fix the free transform tool in AttackPathCanvas.tsx to properly resize text labels when transformation handles are dragged, ensuring font size or text container dimensions update correctly with the visual resize controls

Fix the LoadDiagramDialog.tsx component to display the current date and time for each saved diagram's last modified timestamp, ensuring dates show the actual save time rather than placeholder or incorrect values
Add a user icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar as a draggable element that can be placed on the canvas
Replace the current computer icon in the attack path visualizer with a single computer icon image

new_feature_requests.summary: Create threat hunting report portal interface
Integrate MITRE ATT&CK framework data
Implement PDF report generation
Store threat hunting reports in the backend with fields for report metadata (title, date, author), executive summary, threat actors, selected MITRE ATT&CK techniques, IOCs, and detailed findings
Allow custom text input for MITRE ATT&CK techniques in addition to dropdown selection
Expand report form with more detailed fields needed for comprehensive threat hunting reports
Update the backend Report type to store custom MITRE technique text entries alongside the existing technique IDs, supporting both predefined and custom technique data
Update the backend Report type to include the new detailed threat hunting fields: timeline, attackVector, evidenceCollection, affectedSystems, remediationActions, and recommendations
Create a visual attack path diagram page with drag-and-drop icons for email, attacker, computer, server, domain, IP, and file to illustrate the sequence of attack events
Enable freehand drawing capability on attack path canvas similar to Paint application
Add eraser tool to attack path visualizer for removing drawn elements
Add undo function to attack path visualizer to reverse recent actions
Add text tool to attack path visualizer allowing users to type and place custom text labels
Add specific file type icons to attack path visualizer: .exe, .dll, .pdf, .ppt, .csv, .zip, .doc, and command and control icon
Rename the 'file' icon to 'file/folder' in the attack path visualizer

Implement backend storage for attack path diagrams including all placed icons with positions, connections between icons, freehand drawings, lines, arrows, and text labels with their properties (text content, position, color)
Implement login page using Internet Identity authentication
Add IP address icon back to attack path visualizer toolbar
Extend the backend to support saving complete attack path diagram state including all icons with positions, connections, drawings (freehand, lines, arrows), and text labels with their properties
Change .exe and .dll icons in attack path visualizer to black and white styling
Add a script icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar
Update eraser tool to remove all elements including icons, and enable moving drawing tools
Replace .csv icon with Excel icon and .doc icon with MS Word icon in attack path visualizer
Change IP address icon to display as text 'IP Address' instead of graphical icon
Add an up and down parallel arrow icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar
Enable text label rotation in the attack path visualizer
Add free transform tool to move and resize icons and text labels in the attack path visualizer
Change .exe icon to display as text '.exe' instead of graphical icon
Change Excel icon to display as text 'Microsoft Excel' instead of graphical icon
Change Word icon to display as text 'Microsoft Word' instead of graphical icon
Remove the IP address icon from the attack path visualizer toolbar
Add diagram naming functionality when saving attack path diagrams
Implement diagram selection interface in load functionality to choose from saved diagrams
Add a clear/reset button to the attack path visualizer to remove all elements from the canvas
Replace JPEG export with PNG export functionality for attack path diagrams
Remove the bidirectional arrow icon from the attack path visualizer toolbar
Update the backend diagram storage to support diagram names for saved attack path diagrams
Fix free transform tool to properly resize icons and text labels in attack path visualizer
Add delete functionality to remove saved diagrams from the load diagram interface
Fix eraser tool to delete icons in the attack path diagram
Fix PNG export functionality for attack path diagrams
Fix the free transform tool to properly edit the size of both icons and text labels
Fix the load diagram functionality and set dates to current date and time
Add a user icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar
Update the computer icon in attack path visualizer to display as a single computer icon image
